Operation 'createChatCompletion' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description mentions key features (recursive tool calling, server-side tools) but lacks clarity on inputs, outputs, and execution flow. Added details about message history input, tool execution behavior, and response structure to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Hatz-native chat completion interface with recursive tool calling and server-side tools.
data.summary_suggestion: Generate chat completion with tool calling support
data.description_suggestion: Generates a chat completion response using Hatz AI's native interface. Supports recursive tool calling and server-side tool execution for autonomous agent workflows. Accepts a message history and optional tools/functions. Returns a completion response with generated message content and tool calls. Tool calls are recursively executed server-side until completion or max iterations reached.
data.reasoning: Current description mentions key features (recursive tool calling, server-side tools) but lacks clarity on inputs, outputs, and execution flow. Added details about message history input, tool execution behavior, and response structure to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: OpenAI-compatible wire format responses for clients expecting standard OpenAI response structures.
data.description_suggestion: Generates a chat completion response in OpenAI-compatible wire format for clients expecting standard OpenAI API response structures. Accepts the same message and tool parameters as the native chat completion endpoint. Returns responses formatted identically to OpenAI's API, enabling drop-in compatibility with OpenAI client libraries and integrations. Useful for migrating existing OpenAI-based workflows to Hatz AI.
